title: Key establishment and secure communications using satellite-provided random number values
one_sentence_summary: A system generates cryptographic keys by combining random values from quantum random number generators located on satellites and terrestrial stations, coordinated via key generation parameters to establish secure communication channels.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method where a terrestrial computing system receives first and second random values generated by quantum random number generators, with at least one value provided via satellite communication. These values are combined according to specific key generation parameters to serve as a seed for a cryptographic function that produces a shared symmetric key.
quantum_notes: Quantum Random Number Generators (QRNGs) are explicitly required in independent claims 1 and 12 as the source of the random values used for key generation. The invention relies on quantum-derived entropy provided via satellite or terrestrial QRNGs to ensure high-quality randomness.
independent_claim_subjects: method for generating a cryptographic key based on satellite-provided random values; non-transitory machine-readable medium for generating a cryptographic key

title: Systems and methods for establishing cryptographic keys shared among three or more devices
one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods establish shared cryptographic keys among three or more devices using N-way entangled particle triplets to enable symmetric authentication and secure failover in distributed networks.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method for establishing cryptographic keys shared among three or more devices by generating entangled particle triplets, distributing them to respective host devices, and verifying matching bit sets derived from quantum measurements. This N-way entanglement allows multiple devices to share identical keys without pairwise key exchanges, facilitating secure group messaging and failover capabilities.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is central to the independent claims (1, 15, 18). Claim 1 explicitly requires receiving bits derived from entangled particles. Claims 2 and 16 detail the generation of entangled particle triplets via quantum trisection circuitry. The invention relies on N-way entanglement for key establishment among three or more devices.
independent_claim_subjects: method for establishing cryptographic keys shared among three or more devices; apparatus for establishing cryptographic keys shared among three or more devices; apparatus for establishing a secure communications channel between two or more devices

title: Systems and methods for secure utilization of quick response codes
one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods secure QR code utilization by embedding control sets and keys into payloads, optionally using quantum key distribution via entangled particles to generate shared secrets for authentication.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention secures QR codes by injecting a control set governing applicability (e.g., TTL, bearer identity) into the payload before generating a secure character string. It further encodes a second key within the string using an encoding pattern derived from a shared secret known to both initiating and participating devices.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology (QKD and entangled particles) appears only in dependent claims 2-3, 10-11. The primary invention in independent claims 1, 9, and 17 does not require quantum technology; it functions with any shared key obtained via other means as noted in the description.
independent_claim_subjects: method for secure utilization of quick response (QR) codes; apparatus for secure utilization of quick response (QR) codes; computer program product for secure utilization of quick response (QR) codes

title: Systems and methods for determining a security vulnerability of a computer system
one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods use automated reinforcement learning with large language models to simulate social engineering attacks and identify security vulnerabilities in computer systems.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention integrates a natural language engine into an automated reinforcement learning framework to generate and execute social engineering scripts as actions within a simulated or real environment. This allows the system to learn optimal attack policies by receiving rewards based on successful access to target systems, including those protected by post-quantum cryptography.
quantum_notes: Post-quantum cryptography (PQC) is mentioned only in dependent claim 12, which specifies that the environment may comprise a PQC protected system and rewards are based on accessing it. The primary invention (claims 1, 13, 20) does not require quantum technology or PQC to function; it is an optional embodiment for testing specific security configurations.

title: Secure data processing using data packages generated by edge devices
one_sentence_summary: A method for secure data processing where edge devices generate biometric signatures and encrypt user data with device identifiers to create tamper-evident data packages validated by security tokens.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention generates a data package containing encrypted user data derived from a biometric signature and device identifier, secured by a security token that invalidates upon any alteration. This package is transmitted to secondary systems using digital keys to provide services while ensuring data integrity and authenticity.

title: Systems and methods for identifying activities of individuals in virtual spaces using identity linking
one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods link multiple virtual identities to a foundational identity using identifiers like NFTs or biometrics to verify user activities across different virtual spaces.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention establishes a foundational identity linked to various virtual identities via an identifier such as a string, biometric data, non-fungible token, or image. This linkage enables the verification of identity activities across disparate virtual spaces by associating unlinked identities through the foundational identity.

title: Sensor data processing for monitoring device performance
one_sentence_summary: A system monitors manufactured article status by comparing real-time sound and vibration sensor data against stored identity profiles to detect tampering or performance degradation.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method of maintaining identity elements for manufactured articles by capturing sound and vibration sensor data during normal operations, generating a sound signature, and storing it in an identity profile. Subsequent sensor readings are compared against the stored profile to determine whether the article's state has substantially changed, thereby detecting tampering, substitution, or performance degradation without reliance on traditional security labels or human inspection.

title: Machine-learning for real-time and secure analysis of digital metrics
one_sentence_summary: A machine-learning system analyzes digital identity profiles to detect security risks and generates transitional elements via an AI agent to trigger secure electronic messages.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention trains an artificial intelligence agent using historical digital identity metrics to identify transitional elements indicating security risks. The trained agent then processes new profiles to generate these elements and triggers the transmission of electronic messages containing selectable links for remediation.

title: Translate random unique method for passcode encryption technology
one_sentence_summary: A system authenticates users by deriving unique transaction keys from a base key and counter to encrypt passwords generated from dynamic gesture or biometric inputs mapped to random value tables.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention combines Derived Unique Key Per Transaction (DUKPT) style key derivation with a dynamic, randomized mapping of user gestures or biometrics to generate one-time passcodes. This approach ensures that even if a specific gesture is repeated, the resulting encrypted secret value changes per transaction due to the evolving key and random grid values.

title: Secure generation of authentication datasets from network activity
one_sentence_summary: A system generates secure authentication datasets by analyzing secured and unsecured network data sources using NLP to detect threats and enforce credential replacement.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention utilizes a machine-learning model, specifically natural language processing, to analyze data from both secured and unsecured external sources. It identifies recurrent data elements indicating security threats and automatically forces the generation of new credentials that are not present in the compromised datasets.

title: Authentication using a knowledge factor identification transaction with challenge authentication token
one_sentence_summary: A system authenticates users by generating a password structure combining a static password with a server-provided random challenge sequence to create a challenge authentication token.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention combines a static knowledge factor with a dynamic challenge sequence to generate a unique password structure for each transaction. This structure is embedded in a challenge authentication token along with a user identifier and client timestamp to prevent replay attacks without requiring dedicated hardware or biometrics.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is mentioned only in the description as an optional method for generating the random challenge sequence (using qbits and quantum bases). It does not appear in any independent claims. Claims 3 and 13 mention 'post-quantum cryptography' algorithms generally but do not specify quantum hardware or protocols as required elements of the primary invention.

title: Secure digital authorization via generated datasets
one_sentence_summary: A system generates linked digital identity profiles containing cross-user biometric data and activation conditions to issue security access tokens granting authorized users access to specific digital assets upon trigger events.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a method for secure digital authorization by receiving datasets containing biometric data from multiple users to generate linked identity profiles and specific linkage definitions. These definitions associate activation conditions with digital assets, triggering the generation of security access tokens only when real-time inputs satisfy those conditions. Upon verifying the user's biometric data against the generated profile, the system transmits these tokens to grant authorized access to the secured digital assets.

title: Systems and methods for classical-quantum encryption and decryption
one_sentence_summary: A classical-quantum encryption system encodes plaintext into analytic function coefficients via Taylor series on a classical computer, requiring a quantum computer to reverse the process for decryption.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention creates an asymmetric paradigm where encryption is performed classically by generating a Taylor series expansion of an analytic function derived from a symmetric key and plaintext. Decryption is strictly dependent on a quantum computer to derive the original analytic function from the ciphertext coefficients, leveraging the computational difficulty of inverting Taylor series expansions.
quantum_notes: Quantum computing is central to the invention's decryption phase. While independent claims (1, 6, 11) describe the encryption method/apparatus/product which are classical, the Abstract and Description explicitly define the 'classical-quantum' paradigm where decryption requires a quantum computer to derive the analytic function from the Taylor series coefficients. The text states: 'decryption is performed using a quantum computer' and describes using quantum graph similarity and phase estimation algorithms for this purpose.
independent_claim_subjects: method for classical-quantum encryption; apparatus for classical-quantum encryption; computer program product for classical-quantum encryption

title: System and methods for authentication between multiple devices using N-way entanglement
one_sentence_summary: A system authenticates multiple devices using N-way entangled particles to generate shared keys, enabling secure failover connections when primary links fail without redistributing new keys.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method where a first device generates a key from N-way entangled particles and, upon failing to connect to a second device, establishes a connection with a third device by confirming the third device holds an identical key derived from the same entangled set. This allows seamless failover authentication without generating or distributing new keys.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology (N-way entanglement and QKD) is central to the independent claims (1, 8, 15). The invention requires generating keys based on quantum entangled particles received from a QKD device to enable the authentication and failover mechanism. It appears in all independent claims as a mandatory component.

title: Systems and methods for mobile quantum key distribution
one_sentence_summary: A mobile quantum key distribution system uses a portable device to generate entangled particles and inject them into remote devices or derive secure keys for legacy hardware to facilitate secure communication.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention discloses a mobile quantum key distribution (MQKD) device that physically transports between locations to inject entangled particles directly into quantum-enabled devices. For non-quantum-enabled devices, the MQKD device measures the particles locally to generate and inject classical secure keys, enabling hybrid interoperability.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is central to the independent claims (1, 9, 18). The invention requires generating a pair of entangled particles and injecting quantum data based on these particles. The primary method involves determining if devices are quantum-enabled; if so, entangled particles are transmitted directly. If not, the MQKD device measures the particles to generate classical keys. Thus, quantum generation is required for the core invention, though the transmission of raw quantum states is conditional on device capability.

title: Systems and methods for distributing symmetric keys via a quantum-secure key depot
one_sentence_summary: A quantum-secure key depot uses entangled particles to establish an initial symmetric key with a host, then derives and distributes client keys from encrypted seed data.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention utilizes Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) via entangled particles to generate an initial symmetric key between a fixed key depot and a host device. This initial key secures the transmission of seed data, which the key depot then uses to derive and distribute unique symmetric keys to client devices while notifying the host.
quantum_notes: Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) using entangled particles is the foundational mechanism for generating the initial symmetric key in independent claims 1, 7, and 13. The invention relies on this quantum process to establish the secure channel required for seed data exchange; without it, the specific claimed method of establishing the initial trust anchor does not exist as described.
independent_claim_subjects: method for distributing symmetric keys via QKD and seed derivation; apparatus comprising key depot circuitry for QKD and key derivation; computer program product for quantum-secure key distribution

title: Systems and methods for quantum entangled random key exchange
one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods for secure key exchange across intermediary devices using keys derived from random bit sets, optionally generated via quantum entanglement, to enable end-to-end encryption without exposing keys to intermediaries.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method where a first device derives distinct keys with a second and third device using separate random bit sets. It receives an encrypted message containing a common key from the second device, decrypts it, re-encrypts it with the key shared with the third device, and transmits it to establish secure communication.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology (QERNG) is mentioned in the title, abstract, and description as a source for random bit sets. However, it appears only in dependent claims 4, 5, 11, and 12. The independent claims (1, 8, 15) define the invention using generic 'random bit sets' without requiring quantum generation, making quantum technology an optional embodiment rather than a core requirement.
independent_claim_subjects: method for secure key exchange; device for secure key exchange; computer program product for secure key exchange

title: Digital asset vault
one_sentence_summary: A digital asset vault system that interfaces with multiple third-party wallets to securely transport and store customer secrets using multi-layer encryption and hardware security modules.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a system for receiving data from multiple third-party wallets, encrypting the data at least twice using different encryption techniques provided by those wallets, and storing the encrypted data in a secure vault. It further includes generating keys and managing interfaces for selecting secret types such as cryptocurrency mnemonics.
quantum_notes: Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) is mentioned in the description as a future replacement for RSA and Diffie-Hellman to resist quantum attacks, but it is not claimed in any independent or dependent claims. The primary invention relies on classical algorithms.

title: Systems and methods for hardware security module communication management
one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods manage HSM communication by deriving keys from initial keys and seed bits, optionally using quantum sources, to establish secure groups across different cloud service providers via a key exchange management device.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention establishes secure communication groups between HSMs hosted by different service providers by deriving cryptographic keys from an initial key and seed bits. A key exchange management device facilitates the distribution of these derived keys to merge separate communication groups into a unified secure group.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology (QKD and QRNG) is mentioned in the description as a method for generating initial keys or seed bits. However, it appears only in dependent claims (2, 3, 10, 11, 18, 19). The independent claims (1, 9, 17) define the invention using generic 'initial key' and 'seed bits', making quantum technology an optional embodiment rather than a requirement for the primary invention.

title: Self-contained encrypted data and decryption application for third party data storage and data dissemination
one_sentence_summary: A method bundles encrypted data with a self-contained decryption application containing key portions and credentials to enable secure third-party storage resistant to quantum attacks.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a method for securing data by encrypting an instance using a cryptographic key and technique, then generating a self-contained decryption application that embeds the necessary key portions and credentials. This encrypted data and the custom decryption application are bundled together into a single unit designed to be stored in an external third-party repository. By integrating the decryption logic directly with the data, the system enables secure storage and dissemination while remaining resistant to quantum computing attacks without relying on the third party for key management.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is mentioned in the background as a threat (Shor's algorithm, Grover's algorithm) and as a motivation for using Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC). PQC is explicitly claimed in dependent claims 4, 12, and 19. The independent claims (1, 9, 16) define the invention generally using 'cryptographic technique' without mandating PQC, making quantum resistance an optional embodiment rather than a core requirement of the primary invention.

title: Apparatuses, methods, and computer program products for proactive offline authentication
one_sentence_summary: A system proactively generates offline authentication tokens by comparing current user attributes with prior credentials when a computing device loses network connectivity.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention detects an impending offline condition of a computing device and compares current user attributes from a digital identity construct database against previously generated credentials. title: Extensible quantum random number generation
one_sentence_summary: A system distributes true quantum random numbers from a remote source to devices lacking local generation capability to secure communications via authentication and symmetric encryption.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method where an initiating device without true random number generation functionality obtains a quantum random number from a remote source over a secure channel. This number is then used by security circuitry to generate authentication data or symmetric keys for securing computer-implemented services with a participating device.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is central to the independent claims (1, 9, 14). Claim 1 requires obtaining a 'quantum random number' from a remote source. Claim 14 defines a service device with 'quantum random number generation hardware' using a 'quantum process'. The description also mentions an embodiment using entangled photons for authentication, but the primary claims focus on QRNG distribution.

title: Quantum phenomenon-based obfuscation of memory
one_sentence_summary: A method for securing data by disassembling it into sections and storing them at memory locations determined by a quantum obfuscation map derived from quantum particle states.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention discloses a method where data is disassembled into sections and stored in memory locations assigned via a Quantum Obfuscation Map (QOM). The QOM is generated based on Quantum Obfuscation Elements (QOEs) corresponding to the quantum state of a particle, such as a collapsed wave function or entangled pair.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is central to the independent claims (1, 12, 20). The invention requires a Quantum Obfuscation Map (QOM) generated from Quantum Obfuscation Elements (QOEs) corresponding to a quantum state of a particle. Dependent claims specify QOEs as collapsed wave functions (claim 5) or derived from entangled particles (claim 11). The primary invention relies on these quantum states to determine memory addresses.
independent_claim_subjects: method for storing information using quantum phenomenon-based obfuscation; computing device comprising processing circuitry and cryptographic circuitry; computer program product for storing information using quantum phenomenon-based obfuscation

title: Systems and methods for post-quantum cryptography optimization

title: Systems and methods for quantum consensus
one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods for quantum entanglement authentication generate duplicate session keys at remote devices by measuring correlated subsets of entangled quantum particles to secure communications.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention utilizes a set of entangled quantum particles distributed between two computing devices to generate identical or complimentary numbers based on measurements. These numbers are used to derive matching session keys, authenticating the session without transmitting the key over a classical channel.
quantum_notes: Quantum entanglement is the core mechanism of the invention, appearing in independent claims 1, 19, and 20. The method requires receiving electronic identifications of subsets of entangled quantum particles and generating numbers based on measurements of these particles to authenticate sessions. It is not optional; the primary invention relies entirely on this quantum property.

title: Systems and methods for quantum one-time pad generation
inventors: Carter, Jr.; Robert L. et al.
one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods generate quantum one-time pads using entangled particles stored in separate devices to derive identical random numbers for session authentication and key generation without transmitting the keys.

quantum_notes: Quantum technology is central to the independent claims (1, 18, 19), which require generating and storing entangled quantum particles in quantum storage cells. one_sentence_summary: A system encrypts sensitive personal information with unique random symmetric keys per client and wraps those keys with client public keys to isolate data access in multi-party cloud environments.

title: Methods for protecting data
one_sentence_summary: A method protects sensitive production data by decrypting it, applying homomorphic encryption and masking in a secure middle environment, then storing the masked cleartext in a lower security environment for testing.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention utilizes a three-tier architecture where a secure middle environment acts as a proxy to transform production data. It decrypts encrypted production data, re-encrypts it using homomorphic encryption, applies a masking function, and decrypts the result to generate masked cleartext for lower environments.
quantum_notes: The patent text does not mention quantum technology, quantum cryptography, or post-quantum algorithms. The invention relies on classical homomorphic encryption and symmetric/asymmetric ciphers.

title: Post-quantum cryptography side chain
one_sentence_summary: A method for securing blockchain data by encrypting blocks with classical techniques on a first side chain and re-encrypting them with post-quantum cryptography on a second side chain to mitigate future quantum threats.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention discloses a ledger system where blocks are encrypted using a first cryptographic technique and written to a first side chain, then accessed again to be encrypted with a second cryptographic technique (specifically post-quantum encryption) and written to a second side chain. This dual-layer approach allows for the migration of data security from classical to quantum-resistant algorithms without disrupting the primary blockchain.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is mentioned in the background as a threat (Shor's algorithm, Grover's algorithm) and as the motivation for using post-quantum cryptography. However, the independent claims (1, 10, 19) define the invention using generic 'first' and 'second' cryptographic techniques. The specific requirement that the second technique be 'post quantum encryption' appears only in dependent claims 2, 11, and 20. Therefore, the primary invention is not strictly limited to quantum resistance in its broadest scope, though it is a key embodiment.

threat_model: quantum-attack; harvest now decrypt later; cryptanalysis

title: Systems and methods for post-quantum cryptography communications channels
one_sentence_summary: A system splits electronic communications into overhead and payload portions, transmitting the former over a classical channel and the latter over a post-quantum cryptography channel via an add-on device.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a dual-channel architecture where classical circuitry transmits message overhead to a non-PQC client device while PQC callback circuitry transmits payload data or cryptographic keys to a coupled PQC add-on device. This separation allows legacy systems to migrate to quantum-resistant security by offloading sensitive data transmission to a dedicated PQC channel.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is discussed extensively in the Background and Detailed Description as the threat driver (Shor's algorithm, Grover's algorithm) and motivation for PQC. However, no independent or dependent claims require actual quantum hardware (QKD, QRNG, entanglement). The invention relies on classical 'PQC' algorithms resistant to quantum attacks, not quantum communication channels themselves.

title: Systems and methods for post-quantum cryptography communications channels

primary_inventive_claim: The invention splits an electronic communication into overhead and payload portions. It transmits the overhead classically, uses a quantum cryptographic key distributed over a quantum channel to authenticate the session, and subsequently transmits the payload over a dedicated PQC communications channel.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is central to the independent claims (1, 19, 20), which require transmitting a quantum cryptographic key over a quantum communications channel to authenticate a session. Claim 8 specifies this key as a QKD key. The invention relies on this quantum step before transmitting payload data over a PQC channel.

quantum_notes: Quantum technology is central to the independent claims (1, 19, 20), which require 'quantum cryptographic circuitry' and 'entangled quantum particles' to generate session keys. one_sentence_summary: Systems and methods for quantum entanglement authentication generate duplicate session keys at separate devices by measuring subsets of pre-distributed entangled quantum particles stored in quantum one-time pads.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention utilizes a pair of quantum one-time pads containing entangled particles distributed to two computing devices. By measuring specific subsets of these particles based on transmitted identifiers, both devices generate identical random numbers for session authentication without transmitting the key itself.
quantum_notes: Quantum entanglement is the core mechanism of the invention, appearing in all independent claims (1, 16, 18). title: Systems and methods for post-quantum cryptography communications channels
one_sentence_summary: A system transmits communication overhead over a classical channel while sending payload data encrypted with quantum keys over a separate post-quantum channel to mitigate quantum threats.
primary_inventive_claim: The primary inventive claim describes a system that splits an electronic communication into two distinct portions to mitigate quantum threats by transmitting overhead data over a standard non-PQC channel while sending payload data over a separate post-quantum cryptography channel. This dual-channel approach utilizes the classical circuitry to transmit a PQC indicator data structure that identifies the specific secure channel required for the encrypted payload transmission. By separating the communication overhead from the sensitive payload, the invention ensures that critical data is protected by quantum-resistant encryption without requiring the entire communication stream to traverse the specialized post-quantum infrastructure.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is central to the independent claims (1, 18, 19). Claim 1 explicitly requires 'quantum communications circuitry' to transmit a 'quantum cryptographic key' over a 'quantum communications channel'. The invention relies on this quantum channel for key distribution to secure the payload data transmitted over the PQC channel.

title: Biometric electronic signature tokens
one_sentence_summary: A system generates biometric electronic signature tokens by tokenizing a user's biometric sample, hashing it, binding it to a timestamp, and digitally signing the result with a private key to provide two-factor authentication.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method of generating a biometric electronic signature token by tokenizing a biometric sample, cryptographically binding its hash to a trusted time stamp, and digitally signing both the record and the tokenized sample with a user's private key. This process enables dual-factor authentication combining possession of a private key and inherence of a biometric trait.

title: SYSTEM AND METHOD FOR EXECUTING MACHINE LEARNING MODEL INFERENCE
one_sentence_summary: A system for secure machine learning inference using a trusted processing circuit, public key verification against a database, blind signatures for input data, and usage limits embedded in encrypted models.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a system where a trusted processing circuit decrypts an encrypted machine learning model only after its public key is verified against a database and a zero-knowledge proof confirms possession of the private key. title: Temporary Enablement of Functionality of a Secure Device
one_sentence_summary: A method temporarily enables secure device functionality by generating a unique HMAC key, computing an HMAC with device-unique data, and verifying a signed HMAC from a trusted device using asymmetric cryptography.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention enables temporary functionality on a secure device without storing reusable credentials by generating a transient HMAC key and device-unique data to create a challenge. A trusted device signs this challenge, and the secure device verifies the signature using a stored public key to authorize the temporary enablement.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology (specifically True Random Number Generators compliant with NIST SP 800-90B) is mentioned in the description as a potential source for generating the HMAC key (e.g., 'NIST SP 800-90B compliant true random number generator'). It is not claimed in any independent or dependent claims, nor is it required for the primary invention, which can utilize deterministic random number generators or other methods.
standards_referenced: FIPS 198-1; FIPS 186-5; NIST SP 800-90B; NIST SP 800-90A

title: DECENTRALIZED CONFIGURATION INTEGRITY CHECK IN MICROSERVICES ENVIRONMENT

title: Using a single AES key to encrypt PKCS #7 files
one_sentence_summary: A system encrypts multiple digital certificates and private keys in PKCS #7 files using a single AES key to reduce Hardware Security Module calls during batch provisioning.

title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR ENHANCED DIGITAL SIGNATURE SCHEMES
one_sentence_summary: A system for enhanced digital signatures in PKI using an improved McEliece code-based scheme with Goppa codes to sign certificates while reducing processing and storage requirements.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a digital signature scheme for PKI certificates based on an enhanced McEliece cryptosystem using Goppa codes. It utilizes a random vector derived from a seed to concatenate with a certificate hash, computing a syndrome and codeword to generate a signature that avoids the security and performance limitations of conventional schemes like MGD.
quantum_notes: Quantum computing is mentioned in the Background section as a threat driving the need for post-quantum alternatives, but no quantum technology (QKD, QRNG, etc.) is claimed or required for the invention. The invention itself is a classical post-quantum algorithm (code-based).

title: Peer-to-peer transmission system with a controlled, double-tier cryptographic key structure
one_sentence_summary: A peer-to-peer transmission system using a double-tier cryptographic key structure where service data is encrypted by a consumer key and then re-encrypted by a user key before being stored on a blockchain.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a secure peer-to-peer transmission system utilizing a double-tier encryption scheme where service response data is first encrypted with a data consumer's key and subsequently encrypted with a user's key. This double-encrypted data is stored as an immutable transaction on a blockchain, ensuring that only the user and the specific data consumer can access the information.

title: METHOD AND SYSTEM OF ENCODING DATA OVER DISTRIBUTED NETWORKS AND METHOD OF ASSURING INTEGRITY OF DATA TRANSMISSION BETWEEN SENDER AND RECEIVER IN A COMMUNICATION SYSTEM
one_sentence_summary: A method for encoding data into holographic tokens using fractionalization, encryption, polar coding, and superimposed coding with entanglement to ensure integrity and anti-fragility over distributed networks.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method of encoding data by fractionalizing it into shards, encrypting them, applying polar coding to generate polarized tokens, and dispersing them via superimposed coding to create holographic tokens. These tokens are further intermixed via entanglement to homogenize identities, ensuring the data cannot be cloned and remains resilient against network catastrophes.
quantum_notes: Quantum concepts, specifically 'entanglement' and 'superpositioning', are central to the independent claims (1, 12, 21). Claim 1 requires dispersing tokens using superimposed coding (superpositioning). Claim 5 (dependent on 2) and Claim 15 (dependent on 13) explicitly require intermixing tokens via entanglement to homogenize identities. Claim 21 (independent) explicitly includes intermixing via entanglement as a step in the method. The invention relies on these quantum-inspired principles for its claimed anti-fragility and non-clonability.
independent_claim_subjects: method for encoding data over distributed networks; computer system for encoding data; method of assuring integrity of data transmission

title: Cybersecurity infection detection system and method
one_sentence_summary: A cybersecurity infection detection system using redundant self-monitoring probes and monitors that self-destruct upon compromise, utilizing digital signatures, hashing, and encrypted communication channels to ensure defense in depth.

title: APPARATUS AND METHOD WITH HOMOMORPHIC ENCRYPTION
assignee: Samsung Electronics Co., Ltd.,Seoul National University R&DB Foundation,Industry Academic Cooperation Foundation, Chosun University,Daegu Gyeongbuk Institute of Science and Technology
one_sentence_summary: An apparatus and method for performing homomorphic encryption operations on encrypted images by packing data into extended tensors to maintain computational efficiency during convolution with strides greater than one.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method of generating packed data by mapping an encrypted image to an extended tensor based on a mapping constant derived from tensor dimensions. This packed data is then used in a homomorphic convolution operation, followed by rotation and addition to extract valid values, thereby preventing data density loss associated with strided convolutions.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is mentioned only in the Background section (Description) stating that homomorphic encryption is 'lattice-based and thus, resistant to quantum algorithms'. No independent or dependent claims require or describe quantum technology; the invention is a classical optimization for homomorphic encryption.
independent_claim_subjects: An apparatus comprising processors configured to generate packed data and perform homomorphic encryption; A processor-implemented method comprising generating packed data and performing homomorphic encryption; A processor-implemented method comprising determining a mapping constant and performing a convolution operation

title: TECHNOLOGIES FOR USING NFC OR QR CODE TO COMMISSION A DEVICE TO THE CLOUD
one_sentence_summary: A method for commissioning offline access control devices to a cloud system using NFC or QR codes to retrieve encrypted commissioning data secured by factory-generated cryptographic key pairs.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention enables in-field commissioning of offline access control devices by scanning a unique identifier (NFC/QR) to retrieve cloud-generated commissioning data. This data is encrypted with a public key received from the manufacturer, ensuring only the specific device with the corresponding private key stored in its cryptography circuit can decrypt and apply the configuration.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is mentioned only in the Detailed Description as a potential future embodiment ('one or more quantum-resistant cryptography algorithms may be leveraged... algorithms leveraging a lattice matrix'). It does not appear in any independent or dependent claims, nor is it required for the primary invention.

title: HSM-MEMORY-EFFICIENT MEANS OF PROVIDING HSM-BASED PKI OPERATIONS FOR VEHICLE CONTROLLERS
one_sentence_summary: A memory-efficient method for validating X.509 certificate chains in vehicle HSMs by sequentially processing certificate pairs and storing cryptographic tags for revalidation.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention validates certificate chains in memory-constrained HSMs by storing only a root certificate and a pair of certificates (subordinate and signer) at any time, deleting the signer after verification. It further enables revalidation by storing cryptographic hashes or MACs of validated certificates instead of the full certificate data.

title: TECHNIQUES FOR PROTECTING WEB-ACCESS

title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR DATA AUTHENTICATION USING COMPOSITE KEYS AND SIGNATURES
one_sentence_summary: A system for enhanced PKI using composite and combined digital signatures with multiple keys and algorithms to support hybrid classical and post-quantum authentication.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a method for generating and validating digital certificates containing a composite signature field with multiple signatures derived from different keys and cryptographic algorithms. It enables nested signature structures where subsequent signatures encrypt previous ones, allowing for algorithm agility and hybrid security.

title: Decentralized techniques for managing device administration rights
one_sentence_summary: A decentralized system uses a consensus-based record-keeping network to store immutable transaction records of administrator changes for IoT devices, enabling devices to identify current administrators and fetch firmware without centralized authority.

title: METHOD AND APPARATUS FOR TRANSFERRING DATA IN A PUBLISH-SUBSCRIBE SYSTEM
one_sentence_summary: A method for secure data transfer in a publish-subscribe system combines group key protection with asymmetric point-to-point encryption to ensure end-to-end security between specific devices within a group.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a hybrid security approach where a message is protected by a symmetric group key and simultaneously encrypted for a specific recipient using an asymmetric public key. This allows end-to-end encryption within a group communication channel, preventing other group members from decrypting the message.

title: IDENTITY MANAGEMENT FOR SOFTWARE COMPONENTS
one_sentence_summary: A method for managing software component identities in ephemeral environments using transient one-time credentials to request dynamically created operational certificates without persistent local storage.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention provides a method where a software component instance requests a transient, one-time use identity credential based on a unique identifier. This transient credential is then used to sign a request for a dynamically created second identity credential, enabling cryptographic operations without requiring the component to store private keys persistently.

one_sentence_summary: A third-party authorization system encrypts authorization codes with a public key and verifies digitally signed codes to securely exchange access tokens between systems.

title: Systems and Methods for Hiding Private Cryptographic Keys in Multimedia Files
one_sentence_summary: A system and method for hiding private cryptographic keys, including post-quantum keys, within multimedia files using steganography based on Goppa codes to secure data against quantum computer attacks.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention claims a method for hiding private data in operable multimedia files using steganography implemented via error-correction determined by a Goppa code. It specifically enables the concealment of post-quantum cryptography secret keys within standard media formats while maintaining the appearance of unaltered data to the user.
quantum_notes: Quantum technology is mentioned in the abstract and background as the threat model (quantum computers breaking RSA). The primary invention (claims 1, 7, 13) is a steganographic method using Goppa codes. Quantum relevance appears only in dependent claims 6, 12, and 19, which specify that the hidden private data can be a 'post-quantum cryptography (PQC) secret key'. The core steganographic mechanism does not require quantum technology to function, but is designed to protect keys that are quantum-resistant.
independent_claim_subjects: method for hiding private data in operable data; cryptographic system for hiding private data in multimedia files; method for hiding private data in multimedia files using PIN

title: Smart contract executed within a blockchain
one_sentence_summary: A blockchain-based smart contract system for anonymous loan arrangement where bidders encrypt interest rates with the initiator's public key, allowing off-chain selection and on-chain consensus validation.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention utilizes a distributed smart contract to collect bids containing encrypted parameters (interest rates) and unencrypted partial values (amounts). The initiator decrypts these parameters off-chain to select winning bids based on ranking, then submits the selection to the blockchain for consensus validation by all participants before funds are unlocked.

title: SYSTEMS AND METHODS FOR REGISTERING OR AUTHENTICATING A USER WITH A RELYING PARTY
one_sentence_summary: A method for deterministic on-the-fly generation of elliptic curve key pairs using a stored secret key and relying party data, eliminating persistent private key storage.
primary_inventive_claim: The invention deterministically generates a unique elliptic curve key pair for each relying party using a single stored secret key and request data, then immediately deletes the key pair.
